title: Merck Financial Summary From 2025 Form 10-K
text: Merck's 2025 Form 10-K reported sales of $65.0 billion, compared with $64.2 billion in 2024 and $60.1 billion in 2023. GAAP net income attributable to Merck was $18.3 billion, up 7% from 2024, and non-GAAP net income was $22.5 billion, up 16%. GAAP diluted EPS was $7.28, while non-GAAP diluted EPS was $8.98. The Pharmaceutical segment generated most company sales, with full-year 2025 sales of $58.1 billion, while Animal Health sales were $6.4 billion. Merck also continued shareholder returns, including dividends and share repurchases, and authorized additional share repurchases in 2025. The company acquired Verona Pharma in 2025, adding Ohtuvayre for COPD, and completed the Cidara acquisition in early 2026 to add a late-stage antiviral candidate. Key risks include dependence on KEYTRUDA, future patent and exclusivity loss, vaccine demand declines, regulatory approvals, clinical trial execution, pricing pressure, currency exposure, competition, and integration of acquisitions.
